differentials.name: Seminoma ›  along a totipotential line → forming differentiated nonseminomatous tumors (more aggressive than seminoma):
differentials.raw: Seminoma ›  along a totipotential line → forming differentiated nonseminomatous tumors (more aggressive than seminoma):

differentials.name: Choriocarcinoma
differentials.raw: Choriocarcinoma (extraembryonic differentiation) (b)  mixed lesion ›  from totipotential cells developing along several pathways (embryonal carcinoma being the most common component of mixed lesions)

differentials.name: Teratocarcinoma (= teratoma + embryonal cell ca.) ◊  2nd most common after seminoma, may occasionally undergo spontaneous regression
differentials.raw: Teratocarcinoma (= teratoma + embryonal cell ca.) ◊  2nd most common after seminoma, may occasionally undergo spontaneous regression

differentials.name: serum α-fetoprotein normal in pure seminomas
differentials.raw: serum α-fetoprotein normal in pure seminomas

differentials.name: β-hCG elevation (in 5-25%) ← produced by syncytiotrophoblastic giant cells
differentials.raw: β-hCG elevation (in 5-25%) ← produced by syncytiotrophoblastic giant cells

differentials.name: symptoms of metastatic disease while primary not yet palpable
differentials.raw: symptoms of metastatic disease while primary not yet palpable

differentials.name: serum β-hCG always elevated → gynecomastia in 10% N.B.:   choriocarcinoma has a proclivity for hemorrhage in primary site and metastases!
differentials.raw: serum β-hCG always elevated → gynecomastia in 10% N.B.:   choriocarcinoma has a proclivity for hemorrhage in primary site and metastases!

differentials.name: serum α-fetoprotein elevated in > 90%
differentials.raw: serum α-fetoprotein elevated in > 90% (exclusive to yolk sac elements)

context: Origin:   primitive spermatogenic cell (a)  one histologic type in 65% ›  from unipotential nondifferentiated gonadal line: =  evolution of mixed germ cell tumor → into mature teratoma after chemotherapy (in 40%) → followed by interval growth despite maintaining a benign histology Seminoma (35-50%) ◊  Most common tumor in undescended testis! ◊  Most common pure germ cell tumor! Average age:   40.5 years (slightly older patient compared with other germ cell tumors) Stem cell:   unipotential cell line Histo:   uniform cellular morphology resembling primitive germ cells Presentation:   in 75% lim
